label: Mohenjo-daro
sub: Indus Valley urban center (c. 2500–1700 BCE) — planned streets, Great Bath, advanced drainage

label: Harappa
sub: Type-site of Harappan Civilization (c. 2600–1700 BCE) in modern Punjab, Pakistan

label: Bodh Gaya
sub: Site of Buddha's enlightenment under the Bodhi Tree (c. 528 BCE) — UNESCO World Heritage Site

label: Pataliputra (Patna)
sub: Capital of Maurya (322–185 BCE) and Gupta Empires (320–550 CE) — ancient world's largest city

label: Taxila
sub: Ancient center of learning; major city of Gandhara. Alexander visited 326 BCE. UNESCO World Heritage Site.

label: Varanasi
sub: One of the world's oldest continuously inhabited cities; sacred Hindu city on the Ganges since at least 1200 BCE

label: Nalanda University
sub: World's first residential university (c. 427–1200 CE); drew scholars from China, Korea, and Central Asia

label: Sanchi Stupas
sub: Ashoka-commissioned Buddhist stupas (3rd century BCE) — earliest surviving stone structures in India

label: Hampi (Vijayanagara)
sub: Capital of Vijayanagara Empire (1336–1565); once one of the world's largest cities — UNESCO World Heritage Site

label: Ajanta Caves
sub: Rock-cut Buddhist monastery caves (2nd century BCE – 5th century CE) with the finest ancient Indian paintings

label: Ellora Caves
sub: 34 monastery, temple, and chapel caves (600–1000 CE) representing Hindu, Buddhist, and Jain traditions — UNESCO

label: Panipat
sub: Three decisive battles: 1526 (Mughal conquest), 1556 (Akbar), 1761 (Maratha-Afghan) — 'graveyard of empires'

label: Red Fort, Delhi
sub: Mughal palace-fortress (1639–1648) built by Shah Jahan; site of India's Independence Day speech every August 15

label: Taj Mahal, Agra
sub: Mughal mausoleum built by Shah Jahan (1632–1653) for Mumtaz Mahal — UNESCO World Heritage Site

label: Qutb Minar, Delhi
sub: World's tallest medieval minaret (73m), begun by Qutb-ud-Din Aibak (1193) of the Delhi Sultanate — UNESCO

label: Plassey Battlefield
sub: Site of the Battle of Plassey (June 23, 1757) — East India Company victory establishing British dominance in Bengal

label: Kolkata (Calcutta)
sub: Capital of British India 1773–1911; center of colonial administration, Bengal Renaissance, and 1943 famine

label: Amritsar — Golden Temple
sub: Harmandir Sahib (Golden Temple) — Sikh holiest site; site of 1919 Jallianwala Bagh massacre; 1984 Operation Blue Star

label: Jallianwala Bagh
sub: Site of April 13, 1919 British massacre — Dyer ordered troops to fire on unarmed crowd; 379+ killed (disputed)

label: Sabarmati Ashram, Ahmedabad
sub: Gandhi's base 1917–1930; starting point of the Dandi Salt March (March 12, 1930)

label: Mumbai
sub: Financial capital; last British troops departed Gateway of India (1948); India's largest city and economic hub

label: Pokhran Test Site
sub: Rajasthan desert site of India's nuclear tests: 1974 (Smiling Buddha) and 1998 (Operation Shakti, 5 tests)

label: New Delhi — Parliament
sub: Capital of independent India since 1947; new Parliament building inaugurated by PM Modi, May 2023

label: Srinagar, Kashmir
sub: Largest city of Jammu & Kashmir; at center of India-Pakistan territorial dispute since 1947 Partition

label: Bengaluru — Silicon Valley of India
sub: India's IT hub; home to Infosys, Wipro, TCS, and 1,000+ tech companies; India's fastest-growing major city

label: Lothal
sub: Indus Valley port city (c. 2400–1900 BCE) in Gujarat — world's earliest known dockyard, bead factory, and planned township

label: Fatehpur Sikri
sub: Akbar's purpose-built Mughal capital (1571–1585), abandoned after ~14 years due to water scarcity — UNESCO World Heritage Site

label: Cellular Jail, Port Blair
sub: British colonial 'Kala Pani' prison (built 1906) in Andaman Islands used to exile freedom fighters; now a national memorial

label: Bhopal — Union Carbide Disaster
sub: Site of December 2–3, 1984 gas disaster — world's worst industrial accident; 3,787+ immediate deaths; 15,000–20,000 total estimated

label: Dandi, Gujarat
sub: Endpoint of Gandhi's 241-mile Salt March (April 6, 1930) — civil disobedience against British salt tax that galvanized global opinion
